title: Request normalization

advisory_findings.issue: Claude noted the normalizer does not reject unexpected extra top-level bindingRequest keys.
advisory_findings.adjudication: Rejected as required fix. The output is constructed field-by-field from an explicit allow-list, so extra input keys cannot propagate. This also matches the sibling normalizeControlPlaneClaudeReviewRequest pattern. Optional defense-in-depth is deferred.

advisory_findings.issue: Claude observed rp00:control-plane:validate remains red until S02 evidence files exist.
advisory_findings.adjudication: Accepted as expected closeout sequencing. The final gate is rerun after evidence creation.

review_text: PASS. RP00.P02.M05.S02 implements metadata-only Permission And Audit Binding request normalization exactly to scope. It depends correctly on production_ready RP00.P02.M05.S01 and RP00.P02.M04.S14, normalizes tenant/actor/Matter/permission/audit/role/deny/security-trim refs plus binding contexts, evidence refs, and acceptance gates, fails closed on unsafe inputs and claims, performs no runtime permission, AuthZ, permission-engine, security-trimming, audit-write, service-route, database, storage, product-state, registry, or human-approval replacement behavior, hands off to RP00.P02.M05.S03, and keeps RP00.P02.M05 open.
deferred_or_informational: Optional defense-in-depth could reject unexpected top-level bindingRequest keys, but this is non-blocking because output construction is allow-listed.
The pre-evidence RP00 validator failure is expected and is resolved by authoring this evidence packet and rerunning the gate.

advisory_findings: ADV-1 optional top-level request extra-key rejection
ADV-2 expected pre-evidence RP00 gate failure
